title: Geoeconomics: 'All dependencies, minerals, currencies and semiconductors, are now being weaponized'
subhead: According to geopolitics expert Marc-Antoine Eyl-Mazzega, the competition for control over minerals, logistics and the flow of raw materials is benefiting the US and China. Europe lags behind in this geoeconomic race, with a tangible impact on how major powers are reshaping their defense capabilities.

title: French debt cost climbs to highest level since 2009
Behind the cordial facade, the Trump-Xi summit yields no significant progress
'We don't know if we'll make it back alive': Israel targets Lebanese rescue workers despite ceasefire
subhead: Two civil defense rescue workers were killed by an Israeli airstrike in front of their station in southern Lebanon on Tuesday. More than 110 Lebanese healthcare workers have been killed over two and half months of bombings, including 12 since mid-April, when a supposed ceasefire was declared.

title: Migrant rights: Council of Europe member states push back against the European Court of Human Rights
subhead: Prompted by several governments, including those led by Giorgia Meloni of Italy and Mette Frederiksen  of Denmark, the 46 member states have issued a political declaration aimed at tightening the European Court of Human Rights' (ECHR) case law on migrant rights.

title: Eurovision 2026: Israel's participation sparks controversy
subhead: In an unprecedented move, five countries – Spain, Ireland, Iceland, the Netherlands and Slovenia – are boycotting the event in protest over the presence of an Israeli contestant. Pro-Palestinian demonstrations are also planned in Vienna, where the final will be held on Saturday, May 16.

title: 'People are hiding out at home, some don't even go to work anymore': Residents of El Paso, Texas try to evade ICE agents
subhead: Long present in this border city with Mexico, where 80% of the population is Hispanic, the US immigration enforcement agency has recently stepped up its operations and arrests. It has even opened a 5,000-bed detention center in the neighboring military zone.

How China is cracking down on its Christians
subhead: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

title: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
subhead: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.

title: Military researcher Céline Marangé on the Ukraine war: 'What is truly in danger is the future of European liberal democracy'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the political scientist places the conflict in Ukraine within the long history of Russian imperialism and assesses the threat that the Kremlin poses to NATO and EU countries.
